content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 11, 2003


Caren Daniels-Meade, Chief
Political Reform Division
Office of the Secretary of State
1500 11th Street, Room 495
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-178

Dear Ms. Daniels-Meade:

This is in response to your request for advice concerning your duties as filing officer for campaign statements filed pursuant to the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

What steps must you take if a candidate for Governor in the October 7, 2003, recall election files an amended Candidate Intention (Form 501) to change his or her decision to accept (or decline to accept) the voluntary expenditure limits?

CONCLUSION

Because the amended Form 501 has no force and effect, it should be returned to the candidate.

FACTS

As chief of the Secretary of State's Political Reform Division, you perform the duties of the filing officer for campaign statements filed by state candidates and committees. As a result of the passage of Proposition 34, candidates for elective state office must indicate on the Candidate Intention (Form 501) whether they accept or decline to accept the voluntary expenditure limits established by section 85400. You

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Our File No. A-03-178
Page 2

have requested advice about your filing officer duties if a replacement candidate for
Governor in the October 7, 2003, state recall election files an amended Form 501 to
change his or her designation regarding the voluntary expenditure limits.

ANALYSIS

Under the Act, candidates and committees must file periodic statements
disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with election
activities. (Section 84100, et seq.,) For statements filed by candidates for elective state
office, their controlled committees, and committees that support or oppose state
candidates and ballot measures, the Secretary of State is the filing officer. (Section
84215.) Prior to receiving any contributions, a state candidate also must file with the
Secretary of State a Candidate Intention statement (Form 501). (Section 85200.) Section
81010 imposes the following duties on filing officers:

"(a) Supply the necessary forms and manuals
prescribed by the Commission;
(b) Determine whether required documents have
been filed and, if so, whether they conform on their face
with the requirements of this title;
(c) Notify promptly all persons and known
committees who have failed to file a report or statement in
the form and at the time required by this title;
(d) Report apparent violations of this title to the
appropriate agencies; and
(e) Compile and maintain a current list of all
reports and statements filed with this office."

Proposition 34 added provisions allowing candidates for elective state office to
accept voluntary expenditure limits. (Sections 85400-85403). Candidates who accept the
limits are designated in the ballot information materials and may purchase space there for
a statement. (Sections 85600-85601.)

Candidates must indicate whether they accept the voluntary expenditure limits
when filing the Candidate Intention statement (Form 501) pursuant to section 85200.
(Section 85401(a).) A candidate who does not accept the expenditure limit in a primary
(or special) election may amend the Form 501 within 14 days of the primary (or special)
election to accept the limit for the general (or special runoff) election. (Section
85401(b).) There is no other provision allowing a candidate to amend the Form 501 to
change his or her designation with regard to the voluntary expenditure limits. (Boling
Advice Letter, No. A-01-136.)

In a letter to you dated December 18, 2001, we advised that if a candidate for
elective state office files an amended Form 501 expenditure limit designation other than
as provided in section 85401(b), the amended filing would have no force and effect.

Our File No. A-03-178
Page 3

(Daniels-Meade Advice Letter, No. A-01-285.) We also recommended that the amended
filing be returned to the candidate. You have asked whether the same steps should be
taken if a candidate for Governor in the October 7, 2003, state recall election files an
amended Form 501 expenditure limit designation. Because nothing in the Act would
allow a candidate to amend the expenditure limit designation in connection with a recall
election, we are advising that you should follow the same procedures outlined in our
earlier letter.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
